requested_by: Marcus Webb, VP of Grid Operations
question: Why are tier-2 substations in the West service territory showing 38% more momentary-interruption events vs East, after we rolled out the new IEDs?
metric_label: Momentary interruptions by substation tier and service territory, quarterly
target_grain: one row per (substation_id, tier, service_territory, quarter)

upstream_models.description: Outage events from SCADA and AMI. Includes event_type, duration_sec, substation_id, event_timestamp, and momentary_flag (events under 5 minutes per IEEE 1366).
Substation master with tier classification (1, 2, 3), service territory (East, West, North), IED firmware version, install date, and feeder count.
IED device events from the protection relay log. Includes trip_type, phase_imbalance_pct, firmware_version, and substation linkage. Captures false-trip signatures on phase imbalance.
Service territory master with seasonal load profiles, peak MW by quarter, and regulatory jurisdiction for PUC reporting segmentation.
